37.reason: [Error: When flag==1, j should be incremented after assignment, but using ++j increments before assignment, causing the first character of a new word to be placed at index 1 instead of 0, and subsequent characters to be offset. Also, j is not reset to 0 when a new word starts after a non-alpha character, leading to incorrect indexing.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Error: When flag==1, j should be incremented after assignment, but using ++j increments before assignment, causing the first character of a new word to be placed at index 1 instead of 0, and subsequent characters to be offset. Also, j is not reset to 0 when a new word starts after a non-alpha character, leading to incorrect indexing.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Error: When flag==1, j is pre-incremented, but j was not reset to 0 at the start of a new word, so characters are appended at wrong positions. Also, the first character of a word is stored with j=0 (post-increment in flag==0 case), but subsequent characters use pre-increment, causing the first character to be at index 0 and the second at index 2, skipping index 1.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.125 , reason:  Incorrect index: using pre-increment '++j' skips index j=1, causing a gap in the word string. Should use 'j++' to continue from the current position.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.25 , reason:  [Incorrect index increment; using pre-increment on j causes characters to be placed at positions 2, 3, ... instead of 1, 2, ... skipping index 1. Should be word[i].s[j++]=c.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.125 , reason:  [Logical Error: Strings are not null-terminated with '\0', causing undefined behavior in strcmp and printf.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.2 , reason:  ++j skips index 1, causing non-consecutive character storage; should be j++.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.14285714285714285 , reason:  [When flag != 0, j should not be pre-incremented because j already points to the next position after the last character. This causes skipping index 0 on continuation and potential buffer misalignment.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.2 , reason:  [Index j is incremented before assignment, but j was reset to 0 after non-alpha; however, after a word ends, j is set to 0, then for next word flag=0 so j++ happens correctly. But here, in the else branch (continuing a word), it should be word[i].s[j++] = c;, not ++j before assignment. This causes the first character of a continued word to be skipped or overwritten.]
